steps.title: Model the annual growth
steps.content:
Each year Liam's account has 2.8% more than the previous year:
New Amount = Previous Amount × 1.028

The account value is multiplied by 1.028 each year. This multiplicative pattern—not adding a constant—is the defining characteristic of exponential growth.

steps.title: Recognize exponential, not linear
steps.content: Exponential models multiply by a constant factor each period; linear models add a constant amount each period. Since Liam's account multiplies by 1.028 each year, this is exponential growth.

steps.title: Determine if increasing or decreasing
steps.content: The multiplier 1.028 is greater than 1. When you multiply repeatedly by a number greater than 1, the value grows. This produces increasing exponential growth. That matches choice C — C is correct.
